claims.claim: Stablecoins & onchain payments have product-market fit and enterprise adoption is next; demand is real, uncorrelated usage
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Zero-knowledge proofs / SNARKs / zkVMs (Jolt, sumcheck/GKR/lookups) crossing from theory to practice and compounding
claims.label: confirmed

claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Blockchain scaling & cheap high-quality blockspace (L2s) as key ingredient; more blockspace is good
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Fully onchain games / trustless game servers as a growing niche
claims.label: absent

claims.claim: AI + crypto: autonomous agents that own assets via wallets on credibly neutral rails
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Concerned about memecoins / pure speculation / casino dynamics setting the industry back
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Concerned about company-backed tokens mislabeled as decentralized network tokens
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: ETH as cleanest network token with programmatic value accrual via EIP-1559 burn
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Risk-on / constructive on a multi-year builder horizon; skeptical in analysis, optimistic in action
claims.label: confirmed

missed_changes: Privacy escalated into a dominant standalone theme — repeatedly called 'non-negotiable' / a 'dealbreaker' / 'strict prerequisite' for institutional adoption (RAILGUN, Zcash, Aztec/Noir, Aleo, zkp2p, Seismic, TACEO), going well beyond the prediction's framing of privacy as a sub-benefit of ZK
Newly enthusiastic on real-world-asset tokenization not in the prediction: tokenized equities/stocks, gold (PAXG, which he personally bought), and private credit onchain as 'a big plus for crypto'
New constructive stance on Bitcoin as 'digital gold' / store of value amid rising demand — the prediction held no Bitcoin view
Promoted a new token taxonomy, spotlighting 'arcade tokens' (stable-value, spend-not-speculate utility tokens) as an underappreciated category — a token type absent from the prediction

Sharper, more critical edge on payments readiness: bridges' non-deterministic outputs 'break' cross-chain payments ('I don't think crypto payments can work without this'), and BitPay/KYC checkout called 'an absolute joke' — friction emphasis cutting against the rosy PMF framing
Newly characterized perps / perp dexes as highly-engineered products absorbing speculative/casino demand that previously flowed to memecoins (and tied memecoin cooling to this)
New vocal wallet-security stances: clear-signing absence 'completely unacceptable' (threatening a name-and-shame site) and advocating ERC-20R / reversibility for asset recovery
notes: Eddy stayed remarkably on-thesis: 9 of 10 predicted claims confirmed, none contradicted. The a16z State of Crypto report and podcast (2025-10-22) plus the Empire/network-token podcast (2025-08-22) directly reaffirmed stablecoins/payments, ZK/zkVMs (Jolt), proof-of-personhood, cheap L2 blockspace ('we solved gas'), AI+crypto agent payments (x402), the memecoin-casino concern, the network-token vs company-backed-token distinction, ETH's burn-based value capture, and his risk-on builder optimism ('crypto grew up... app time soon'). The one gap: fully onchain games / EVE Frontier went entirely unmentioned in the window (absent, not contradicted). Biggest unanticipated developments were how heavily privacy dominated his messaging, fresh enthusiasm for RWA tokenization and Bitcoin-as-digital-gold, the 'arcade token' taxonomy, and a notably more critical tone on current payments/wallet UX despite overall bullishness. Judged on stance; consistent with his stated refusal to make price calls.
